title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: elcf4R now supports four household-oriented public data sources common normalized panel schema: elcf4r_read_iflex() elcf4r_read_storenet() elcf4r_read_lcl() elcf4r_read_refit() package also ships compact example panels saved benchmark results main vignettes run without external downloads. Raw source files stay data-raw/ redistributed package unless compact derived artifact explicitly built saved. Two additional unshipped scaffolds also available: elcf4r_download_ideal() / elcf4r_read_ideal() aggregate-electricity hourly summaries IDEAL. elcf4r_download_gx() / elcf4r_read_gx() GX transformer/community-level dataset.

title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: current dataset surface : four household readers return core columns: dataset entity_id timestamp date time_index y temp dow month resolution_minutes Dataset-specific metadata columns preserved available.

title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: IDEAL GX intentionally documented separately core shipped household matrix. Notes: IDEAL support release limited aggregate electricity attempt parse raw 1 Hz stream. GX individual-household dataset. useful secondary benchmark source weather community-level demand behavior, folded package’s core household benchmark claims.

title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: shipped examples small normalized panels intended package examples vignette code. objects can passed directly : elcf4r_build_daily_segments() elcf4r_build_benchmark_index() elcf4r_benchmark()
code: example_sizes <- Filter(   Negate(is.null),   list(     .elcf4r_example_size_row("elcf4r_iflex_example", "iflex"),     .elcf4r_example_size_row("elcf4r_storenet_example", "storenet"),     .elcf4r_example_size_row("elcf4r_lcl_example", "lcl"),     .elcf4r_example_size_row("elcf4r_refit_example", "refit")   ) )  if (length(example_sizes) == 0L) {   data.frame() } else {   do.call(rbind, example_sizes) } #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows

title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: supported household dataset now saved benchmark-result object built fixed local cohort deterministic rolling-origin design. shipped benchmark tables poster-style artifacts. intended replace full local benchmarking raw datasets.
code: benchmark_summary <- Filter(   Negate(is.null),   list(     .elcf4r_benchmark_summary("elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results", "iflex"),     .elcf4r_benchmark_summary("elcf4r_storenet_benchmark_results", "storenet"),     .elcf4r_benchmark_summary("elcf4r_lcl_benchmark_results", "lcl"),     .elcf4r_benchmark_summary("elcf4r_refit_benchmark_results", "refit")   ) )  if (length(benchmark_summary) == 0L) {   data.frame() } else {   benchmark_summary <- do.call(rbind, benchmark_summary)   benchmark_summary[, c("dataset", "method", "nmae", "nrmse", "smape", "mase")] } #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows

title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
text: shipped dataset reproducible data-raw/ script: data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_subsets.R data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results.R data-raw/elcf4r_storenet_artifacts.R data-raw/elcf4r_lcl_artifacts.R data-raw/elcf4r_refit_artifacts.R general pattern : Place original raw files data-raw/. Read elcf4r_read_*(). Build normalized day index elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(). Save compact example panel. Run elcf4r_benchmark() fixed cohort save result table. keeps package lightweight making shipped examples benchmark summaries reproducible.

what: Example: daily segments from a shipped panel
title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
code: if (exists("elcf4r_iflex_example", inherits = FALSE)) {   iflex_segments <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(     elcf4r_iflex_example,     carry_cols = c("dataset", "participation_phase", "price_signal")   )    dim(iflex_segments$segments)   head(iflex_segments$covariates[, c("entity_id", "date", "temp_mean", "price_signal")]) } else {   data.frame() } #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows

what: Example: rerun a tiny benchmark locally
title: Datasets and shipped artifacts
code: if (exists("elcf4r_lcl_example", inherits = FALSE)) {   tiny_index <- elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(     elcf4r_lcl_example,     carry_cols = "dataset"   )    tiny_benchmark <- elcf4r_benchmark(     panel = elcf4r_lcl_example,     benchmark_index = tiny_index,     methods = c("gam", "kwf"),     cohort_size = 1,     train_days = 10,     test_days = 2,     include_predictions = FALSE   )    tiny_benchmark$results[, c("entity_id", "method", "test_date", "nmae", "mase")] } else {   data.frame() } #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: vignette shows complete lightweight workflow using datasets ship package: elcf4r_iflex_example preprocessing model fitting examples. elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results inspecting saved benchmark results.

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: work one participant keep example compact.
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] )  daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(   id1,   carry_cols = c("participation_phase", "price_signal") )  dim(daily$segments) #> [1] 14 24 head(daily$covariates[, c("date", "dow", "temp_mean", "participation_phase")]) #>                         date       dow temp_mean participation_phase #> Exp_1__2020-01-06 2020-01-06    Monday  7.508333             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-07 2020-01-07   Tuesday  7.254167             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-08 2020-01-08 Wednesday  6.312500             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-09 2020-01-09  Thursday  3.420833             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-10 2020-01-10    Friday  1.445833             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-11 2020-01-11  Saturday  6.754167             Phase_1

what: Fit forecasting models on the example panel
title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: train first 10 days predict 11th day. LSTM example available keras3 tensorflow packages installed configured:
code: train_days <- daily$covariates$date[1:10] test_day <- daily$covariates$date[11]  train_long <- subset(id1, date %in% train_days) test_long <- subset(id1, date == test_day)  fit_gam <- elcf4r_fit_gam(   train_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")],   use_temperature = TRUE ) pred_gam <- predict(   fit_gam,   newdata = test_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")] )  fit_mars <- elcf4r_fit_mars(   train_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")],   use_temperature = TRUE ) pred_mars <- predict(   fit_mars,   newdata = test_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")] )  fit_kwf <- elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) pred_kwf <- predict(fit_kwf)  fit_kwf_clustered <- elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) pred_kwf_clustered <- predict(fit_kwf_clustered)  naive_day <- as.numeric(daily$segments[10, ])  rbind(   gam = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_gam, naive_pred = naive_day)),   mars = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_mars, naive_pred = naive_day)),   kwf = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_kwf, naive_pred = naive_day)),   kwf_clustered = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_kwf_clustered, naive_pred = naive_day)) ) #>                    nmae     nrmse     smape      mase #> gam           0.1961463 0.2355394 0.1796715 0.9274129 #> mars          0.1690549 0.1920249 0.1600477 0.7993201 #> kwf           0.1404839 0.1821267 0.1394477 0.6642319 #> kwf_clustered 0.1640983 0.1907997 0.1529166 0.7758848 fit_lstm <- elcf4r_fit_lstm(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   use_temperature = TRUE,   epochs = 1,   units = 4,   batch_size = 2,   verbose = 0 )  pred_lstm <- predict(fit_lstm) unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_lstm, naive_pred = naive_day)) #>      nmae     nrmse     smape      mase  #> 0.2247825 0.2573687 0.2088922 1.0628101 "LSTM example skipped because a working Keras/TensorFlow backend is not available in this R environment."

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: package also exposes reusable rolling-origin benchmark runner works normalized panels produced elcf4r_read_*() adapters.
code: benchmark_index <- elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   carry_cols = c("dataset", "participation_phase", "price_signal") )  benchmark_small <- elcf4r_benchmark(   panel = elcf4r_iflex_example,   benchmark_index = benchmark_index,   methods = c("gam", "kwf"),   cohort_size = 1,   train_days = 10,   test_days = 2,   include_predictions = FALSE )  benchmark_small$results #>                                 benchmark_name dataset entity_id method #> 1 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    gam #> 2 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    kwf #> 3 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    gam #> 4 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    kwf #>    test_date train_start  train_end train_days test_points use_temperature #> 1 2020-01-16  2020-01-06 2020-01-15         10          24            TRUE #> 2 2020-01-16  2020-01-06 2020-01-15         10          24            TRUE #> 3 2020-01-17  2020-01-07 2020-01-16         10          24            TRUE #> 4 2020-01-17  2020-01-07 2020-01-16         10          24            TRUE #>   thermosensitive     thermosensitivity_status thermosensitivity_ratio #> 1              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 2              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 3              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 4              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #>   fit_seconds status error_message      nmae     nrmse     smape      mase #> 1       0.018     ok          <NA> 0.1961463 0.2355394 0.1796715 0.9274129 #> 2       0.012     ok          <NA> 0.1404839 0.1821267 0.1394477 0.6642319 #> 3       0.018     ok          <NA> 0.3692809 0.4462295 0.1641611 1.3876600 #> 4       0.012     ok          <NA> 0.5393512 0.6402973 0.2242763 2.0267394

title: Authors and Citation
text: Frederic Bertrand. Maintainer, author. Fatima Fahs. Author. Myriam Maumy-Bertrand. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Frederic Bertrand, Fatima Fahs Myriam Maumy-Bertrand (2026). Electricity Load Curves Forecasting Individual Level, R package version 0.4.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level},   author = {Frederic Bertrand and Fatima Fahs and Myriam Maumy-Bertrand},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.0},   url = {https://fbertran.github.io/eclf4R/}, }

what: Frédéric Bertrand, Fatima Fahs and Myriam Maumy
title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: Implements Kernel Wavelet Functional (KWF), clustered KWF, Generalized Additive Models (GAM), Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) RNN LSTM models forecast individual electricity load curves, following methodology described Fahs (2023) related articles posters. Includes normalized dataset adapters iFlex, StoreNet, Low Carbon London REFIT, scaffolded download/read support IDEAL GX, compact shipped example panels, saved benchmark artifacts. site created F. Bertrand examples reproduced created F. Bertrand, F. Fahs M. Maumy.

title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: can install latest version eclf4R package github :
code: devtools::install_github("fbertran/eclf4R")

title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: exported forecasting methods currently covered package : elcf4r_fit_gam() elcf4r_fit_mars() elcf4r_fit_kwf() elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered() elcf4r_fit_lstm() current dataset adapters shipped benchmark artifacts cover: iFlex StoreNet (H6_W) Low Carbon London REFIT current download helpers : elcf4r_download_elmas() elcf4r_download_storenet() elcf4r_download_ideal() elcf4r_download_gx() Scaffolded, unshipped dataset adapters: IDEAL: elcf4r_download_ideal() elcf4r_read_ideal() provide first-pass aggregate-electricity scaffold built around hourly summaries auxiliarydata.zip. current Edinburgh DataShare record states CC   4.0. IDEAL-derived package dataset shipped release. GX: elcf4r_download_gx() elcf4r_read_gx() provide secondary transformer/community-level scaffold official figshare dataset record. GX treated part package’s core individual-household benchmark set, GX-derived package dataset shipped release. Licence terms rechecked official dataset record redistribution. current unshipped scaffold readers : elcf4r_read_ideal() elcf4r_read_gx()

title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: package now ships compact example panels saved benchmark results iFlex, StoreNet, Low Carbon London REFIT, main documentation can run without external downloads. artifacts derived local raw files reproducible scripts data-raw/. current shipped benchmark artifacts : iFlex: 15 households, 28 train days, 7 test days StoreNet: 1 household (H6_W), 5 train days, 2 test days Low Carbon London: 1 thermosensitive household, 56 train days, 7 test days REFIT: 2 thermosensitive households, 56 train days, 7 test days

what: Forecasting Individual Electricity Load Curves — elcf4R-package
title: Forecasting Individual Electricity Load Curves — elcf4R-package
text: elcf4R provides methods supporting workflows day-ahead forecasting individual electricity load curves. current package surface includes Kernel Wavelet Functional models, clustered KWF, GAM, MARS LSTM estimators, dataset adapters iFlex, StoreNet, Low Carbon London REFIT, scaffolded download/read support IDEAL GX, helpers build daily segments, rolling-origin benchmarking utilities.

title: Forecasting Individual Electricity Load Curves — elcf4R-package
text: Maintainer: Frederic Bertrand frederic.bertrand@lecnam.net (ORCID) Authors: Fatima Fahs fatima.fahs@es.fr Myriam Maumy-Bertrand myriam.maumy@ehesp.fr (ORCID)

what: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
title: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
text: Evaluate package forecasting methods normalized panel using deterministic rolling-origin design. runner supports current temperature-aware gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered lstm wrappers returns aggregate scores , optionally, saved point forecasts.

title: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
code: elcf4r_benchmark(   panel,   benchmark_index = NULL,   methods = NULL,   entity_ids = NULL,   cohort_size = NULL,   train_days = 28L,   test_days = 5L,   benchmark_name = NULL,   dataset = NULL,   use_temperature = TRUE,   method_args = NULL,   include_predictions = TRUE,   thermosensitivity_panel = NULL,   benchmark_index_carry_cols = NULL,   seed = NULL,   tz = "UTC" )

title: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
text: panel Normalized panel data, typically returned one elcf4r_read_*() adapters. benchmark_index Optional day-level index. NULL, created elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(). methods Character vector method names evaluate. Supported values "gam", "mars", "kwf", "kwf_clustered" "lstm". NULL, runner uses gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered adds lstm backend available. entity_ids Optional character vector entity IDs benchmark. cohort_size Optional maximum number eligible entities keep sorting entity_id. train_days Number days training window. test_days Number one-day rolling test origins per entity. benchmark_name Optional benchmark identifier. NULL, one derived dataset label benchmark design. dataset Optional dataset label overriding unique(panel$dataset). use_temperature Logical; TRUE, methods support temperature use non-missing temperature information available current window. method_args Optional named list per-method argument overrides. include_predictions Logical; TRUE, return long table saved point forecasts naive forecasts. thermosensitivity_panel Optional normalized panel used thermosensitivity classification. Defaults panel. benchmark_index_carry_cols Optional carry_cols passed elcf4r_build_benchmark_index() benchmark_index supplied. seed Optional integer seed forwarded methods support user-supplied seeding, LSTM, unless overridden method_args. tz Time zone used derive dates within-day positions.

title: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
text: object class elcf4r_benchmark elements results, predictions, cohort_index, spec backend.

title: Run a rolling-origin benchmark on a normalized panel — elcf4r_benchmark
code: bench <- elcf4r_benchmark(   panel = elcf4r_iflex_example,   methods = c("gam", "kwf"),   cohort_size = 1,   train_days = 10,   test_days = 2,   include_predictions = FALSE ) head(bench$results) #>                                 benchmark_name dataset entity_id method #> 1 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    gam #> 2 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    kwf #> 3 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    gam #> 4 iflex_hourly_1_ids_10_train_2_test_2_methods   iflex     Exp_1    kwf #>    test_date train_start  train_end train_days test_points use_temperature #> 1 2020-01-16  2020-01-06 2020-01-15         10          24            TRUE #> 2 2020-01-16  2020-01-06 2020-01-15         10          24            TRUE #> 3 2020-01-17  2020-01-07 2020-01-16         10          24            TRUE #> 4 2020-01-17  2020-01-07 2020-01-16         10          24            TRUE #>   thermosensitive     thermosensitivity_status thermosensitivity_ratio #> 1              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 2              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 3              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 4              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #>   fit_seconds status error_message      nmae     nrmse     smape      mase #> 1       0.052     ok          <NA> 0.1961463 0.2355394 0.1796715 0.9274129 #> 2       0.021     ok          <NA> 0.1404839 0.1821267 0.1394477 0.6642319 #> 3       0.019     ok          <NA> 0.3692809 0.4462295 0.1641611 1.3876600 #> 4       0.013     ok          <NA> 0.5393512 0.6402973 0.2242763 2.0267394

what: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
title: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
text: Create compact day-level index normalized panel. returned object contains one row per complete entity-day can reused define deterministic benchmark cohorts without shipping full panel.

title: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
code: elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(   data,   carry_cols = NULL,   id_col = "entity_id",   timestamp_col = "timestamp",   value_col = "y",   temp_col = "temp",   resolution_minutes = NULL,   complete_days_only = TRUE,   drop_na_value = TRUE,   tz = "UTC" )

title: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
text: data Normalized panel data, typically returned one elcf4r_read_*() adapters. carry_cols Optional character vector additional day-level columns propagate benchmark index. NULL, non-core columns carried. id_col Name entity identifier column. timestamp_col Name timestamp column. value_col Name load column. temp_col Name temperature column. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. NULL, inferred data. complete_days_only Passed elcf4r_build_daily_segments(). drop_na_value Passed elcf4r_build_daily_segments(). tz Time zone used derive dates within-day positions.

title: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
text: day-level data frame suitable elcf4r_benchmark().

title: Build a day-level benchmark index from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_benchmark_index
code: idx <- elcf4r_build_benchmark_index(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   carry_cols = c("dataset", "participation_phase", "price_signal") ) head(idx) #>             day_key entity_id       date       dow month temp_mean temp_min #> 1 Exp_1__2020-01-06     Exp_1 2020-01-06    Monday    01  7.508333      6.4 #> 2 Exp_1__2020-01-07     Exp_1 2020-01-07   Tuesday    01  7.254167      5.8 #> 3 Exp_1__2020-01-08     Exp_1 2020-01-08 Wednesday    01  6.312500      4.2 #> 4 Exp_1__2020-01-09     Exp_1 2020-01-09  Thursday    01  3.420833      0.6 #> 5 Exp_1__2020-01-10     Exp_1 2020-01-10    Friday    01  1.445833     -0.4 #> 6 Exp_1__2020-01-11     Exp_1 2020-01-11  Saturday    01  6.754167      4.4 #>   temp_max dataset participation_phase price_signal n_points #> 1      8.7   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24 #> 2      9.7   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24 #> 3     10.3   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24 #> 4      5.5   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24 #> 5      4.7   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24 #> 6      8.8   iflex             Phase_1         <NA>       24

what: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: Convert long-format load table one row per entity-day one column per within-day time index. matrix representation required functional load-curve models rolling benchmark scripts.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
code: elcf4r_build_daily_segments(   data,   id_col = "entity_id",   timestamp_col = "timestamp",   value_col = "y",   temp_col = "temp",   carry_cols = NULL,   expected_points_per_day = NULL,   resolution_minutes = NULL,   complete_days_only = TRUE,   drop_na_value = TRUE,   tz = "UTC" )

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: data Data frame containing least entity id, timestamp load. id_col Name entity identifier column. timestamp_col Name timestamp column. value_col Name load column. temp_col Optional name temperature column used derive day summaries. carry_cols Optional day-level columns propagate returned covariate table. first non-missing value within day kept. expected_points_per_day Expected number samples per day. NULL, derived resolution_minutes. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. NULL, inferred timestamps resolution_minutes column. Fractional minute values allowed. complete_days_only TRUE, incomplete duplicated days dropped output. drop_na_value TRUE, days missing load values dropped. tz Time zone used derive dates within-day positions.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: list components segments, covariates, resolution_minutes points_per_day.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1, carry_cols = "participation_phase") dim(daily$segments) #> [1] 14 24 names(daily$covariates) #>  [1] "day_key"             "entity_id"           "date"                #>  [4] "n_points"            "n_unique_index"      "has_duplicate_index" #>  [7] "has_missing_value"   "dow"                 "month"               #> [10] "temp_mean"           "temp_min"            "temp_max"            #> [13] "participation_phase"

code: elcf4r_calendar_groups(   as.Date(c("2024-12-24", "2024-12-25", "2024-12-26")),   holidays = as.Date("2024-12-25") ) #> [1] pre_holiday holiday     thursday    #> 9 Levels: monday < tuesday < wednesday < thursday < friday < ... < holiday

what: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
text: Estimate thermosensitivity using residential thesis rule based ratio mean winter load mean summer load.

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
code: elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity(   data,   id_col = "entity_id",   date_col = "date",   value_col = "y",   threshold = 1.5,   winter_months = c(12L, 1L, 2L),   summer_months = c(6L, 7L, 8L) )

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
text: data Data frame containing least identifier, date load column. Long-format panels accepted aggregated mean daily load classification. id_col Name entity identifier column. date_col Name date column. value_col Name load column. threshold Ratio threshold series classified thermosensitive. Defaults 1.5. winter_months Integer vector winter months. summer_months Integer vector summer months.

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
text: data frame one row per entity columns winter_mean, summer_mean, ratio, thermosensitive, status.

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
code: example_ts <- data.frame(   entity_id = rep("home_1", 4),   date = as.Date(c("2024-01-10", "2024-01-11", "2024-07-10", "2024-07-11")),   y = c(12, 11, 6, 5) ) elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity(example_ts) #>   entity_id winter_mean summer_mean    ratio threshold thermosensitive status #> 1    home_1        11.5         5.5 2.090909       1.5            TRUE     ok #>   n_winter_days n_summer_days #> 1             2             2

code: elcf4r_fit_gam(data, use_temperature = FALSE)

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
text: data Data frame columns y (load), time_index (numeric factor within day position), dow, month, optional temp covariates. use_temperature Logical. TRUE, include temperature smooth effect interactions.

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "gam".

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) train_data <- subset(id1, date < sort(unique(id1$date))[11]) test_data <- subset(id1, date == sort(unique(id1$date))[11]) fit <- elcf4r_fit_gam(train_data[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")], TRUE) pred <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")]) length(pred) #> [1] 24

what: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: Fit day-ahead Kernel Wavelet Functional (KWF) model ordered daily load curves. implementation computes wavelet-detail distances historical context days, applies Gaussian kernel weights, restricts weights matching calendar groups available, can apply approximation/detail correction used mean-level non-stationarity.

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
code: elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   target_covariates = NULL,   use_temperature = FALSE,   wavelet = "la12",   bandwidth = NULL,   use_mean_correction = TRUE,   group_col = NULL,   holidays = NULL,   weights = NULL,   recency_decay = NULL,   temperature_bandwidth = NULL )

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves (rows days, columns within-day time points) chronological order. covariates Optional data frame one row per training segment. present, function looks deterministic grouping information context_group, kwf_group, calendar_group, column named group_col. explicit group column present, groups derived date holidays, dow fallback. target_covariates Optional one-row data frame describing day forecast. contains date, previous day used context day calendar grouping, matches residential KWF protocol pre-holiday handling. use_temperature Deprecated ignored. Kept backward compatibility earlier package examples. wavelet Wavelet filter name passed wavelets::dwt(). Defaults "la12", least-asymmetric filter aligned thesis implementation. series short requested filter, function falls back "haar". bandwidth Optional positive bandwidth Gaussian kernel wavelet distances. NULL, inferred distances last observed segment. use_mean_correction Logical; TRUE, apply approximation/detail correction used mean-level non-stationarity. group_col Optional column name containing precomputed KWF groups covariates target_covariates. holidays Optional vector holiday dates used elcf4r_calendar_groups() deterministic groups derived date. weights Optional numeric prior weights length nrow(segments). first nrow(segments) - 1 values used historical pairing step. recency_decay Optional non-negative recency coefficient applied exponential prior historical context days. temperature_bandwidth Deprecated ignored. Kept backward compatibility older examples.

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "kwf".

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1, carry_cols = "participation_phase") fit <- elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, ],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) length(predict(fit)) #> [1] 24

what: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: Cluster dyadically resampled daily curves wavelet-energy feature space use resulting cluster labels grouping structure inside KWF forecast.

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
code: elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   target_covariates = NULL,   wavelet = "la12",   bandwidth = NULL,   use_mean_correction = TRUE,   max_clusters = 10L,   nstart = 30L,   cluster_seed = NULL,   weights = NULL,   recency_decay = NULL,   clustering = NULL )

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves chronological order. covariates Optional data frame one row per segment. target_covariates Optional one-row data frame target day. wavelet Wavelet filter name passed wavelets::dwt(). Defaults "la12". bandwidth Optional positive bandwidth Gaussian kernel underlying KWF fit. use_mean_correction Logical; TRUE, apply approximation/detail correction underlying KWF fit. max_clusters Maximum number candidate clusters considered Sugar jump heuristic. nstart Number random starts kmeans. cluster_seed Deprecated ignored. Clustered KWF now uses deterministic non-random starts. weights Optional prior weights passed base KWF fit. recency_decay Optional recency prior passed base KWF fit. clustering Optional elcf4r_kwf_clusters object. supplied, stored clustering model reused instead refit.

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "kwf_clustered".

what: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: LSTM implementation uses one previous daily curves predict next daily curve. use_temperature = TRUE temp_mean available covariates, daily mean temperature added second input feature repeated across within-day time steps.

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
code: elcf4r_fit_lstm(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   use_temperature = FALSE,   lookback_days = 1L,   units = 16L,   epochs = 10L,   batch_size = 8L,   validation_split = 0,   seed = NULL,   verbose = 0L )

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves (rows days, columns time points). covariates Optional data frame one row per training day. use_temperature Logical. TRUE, use temp_mean covariates additional input feature available. lookback_days Number past daily curves used one training input. units Number hidden units LSTM layer. epochs Number training epochs. batch_size Batch size used keras3::fit(). validation_split Validation split passed keras3::fit(). seed Optional integer seed passed TensorFlow. NULL, current backend RNG state used. verbose Verbosity level passed keras3::fit() predict().

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "lstm".

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
code: if (interactive() &&     requireNamespace("keras3", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("tensorflow", quietly = TRUE) &&     suppressWarnings(       tryCatch(         utils::getFromNamespace("is_keras_available", "keras3")(),         error = function(e) FALSE       )     )) {   id1 <- subset(     elcf4r_iflex_example,     entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1]   )   daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1)   fit <- elcf4r_fit_lstm(     segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],     covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, ],     use_temperature = TRUE,     epochs = 1,     units = 4,     batch_size = 2,     verbose = 0   )   length(predict(fit)) }

what: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: Saved benchmark results deterministic rolling-origin evaluation subset iFlex data. shipped results use fixed participant cohort, 28-day training window multiple one-day rolling test forecasts per participant. current shipped benchmark includes operational gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered lstm wrappers.

title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: data frame 20 variables: benchmark_name Identifier benchmark design. dataset Dataset label, always "iflex". entity_id Participant identifier. method Forecasting method: gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered lstm. test_date Date forecast target day. train_start First day training window. train_end Last day training window. train_days Number training days. test_points Number hourly points target day. use_temperature Logical flag temperature-aware fitting. thermosensitive Thermosensitivity flag seasonal coverage sufficient, otherwise NA. thermosensitivity_status Status winter/summer ratio classification step. thermosensitivity_ratio Estimated winter/summer mean-load ratio available. fit_seconds Elapsed fit--predict time seconds. status Benchmark execution status. error_message Error message fit failed. nmae Normalized mean absolute error. nrmse Normalized root mean squared error. smape Symmetric mean absolute percentage error. mase Mean absolute scaled error.

title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: Derived elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index public iFlex raw file data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results.R.

what: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
text: Convert raw long-format load table normalized panel uses column names expected package examples model wrappers.

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
code: elcf4r_normalize_panel(   data,   id_col,   timestamp_col,   load_col,   temp_col = NULL,   dataset = NA_character_,   resolution_minutes = NULL,   tz = "UTC",   keep_cols = NULL )

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
text: data Data frame containing least entity identifier, time stamp load column. id_col Name entity identifier column. timestamp_col Name timestamp column. load_col Name load column. temp_col Optional name temperature column. dataset Short dataset label stored normalized output. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. NULL, inferred timestamps. Fractional minute values allowed high-frequency data. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. keep_cols Optional character vector extra source columns keep.

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
text: data frame normalized columns dataset, entity_id, timestamp, date, time_index, y, temp, dow, month resolution_minutes, plus requested keep_cols.

title: Read and normalize the GX residential transformer-level scaffold — elcf4r_read_gx
text: path Path GX SQLite database, flat export file, directory containing one . ids Optional vector GX community/profile identifiers keep. start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. Defaults "Asia/Shanghai". n_max Optional maximum number rows read. drop_na_load Logical; TRUE, rows missing load values dropped.

title: Read and normalize the GX residential transformer-level scaffold — elcf4r_read_gx

title: Read and normalize the IDEAL hourly aggregate-electricity scaffold — elcf4r_read_ideal

text: path Path data_hourly.csv directory contains . ids Optional vector participant identifiers keep. start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. Defaults "UTC" iFlex timestamps stored trailing Z. n_max Optional maximum number rows read. Intended quick prototyping small subset raw file.

what: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
text: Read one CLEAN_House*.csv files REFIT dataset, optionally select appliance channels, resample regular time grid, return normalized long-format panel.

title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
code: elcf4r_read_refit(   path = "data-raw",   house_ids = NULL,   channels = "Aggregate",   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   tz = "UTC",   resolution_minutes = 1L,   agg_fun = c("mean", "sum", "last"),   n_max = NULL,   drop_na_load = TRUE )

title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
text: path Path REFIT file directory containing CLEAN_House*.csv files. house_ids Optional vector house identifiers keep. matched file stems "CLEAN_House1". channels Character vector load channels extract. Defaults "Aggregate". start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. resolution_minutes Target regular resolution minutes normalized output. Defaults 1. agg_fun Aggregation used resampling target grid. One "mean", "sum" "last". n_max Optional maximum number raw rows read per file. drop_na_load Logical; TRUE, rows missing load values dropped resampling.

title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
text: normalized data frame REFIT household data.

title: Read and normalize the StoreNet household dataset — elcf4r_read_storenet
text: path Path StoreNet CSV file directory containing files named like H6_W.csv. ids Optional vector household identifiers keep. Identifiers matched file stem, example "H6_W". start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. n_max Optional maximum number rows read per file. load_col Name load column normalize. Defaults "Consumption(W)". keep_cols Optional extra source columns keep. Defaults main battery production fields present.

title: Read and normalize the StoreNet household dataset — elcf4r_read_storenet
text: normalized data frame StoreNet household data.
